message:
feat(config): extract TOML config parsing, add proptest round-trip coverage and config benchmarks

Extracted parse_toml_config_with_overrides() from the file-loading path
so that TOML parsing + override merging + validation can be tested and
benchmarked without filesystem dependencies. The override merge logic
was deduplicated into merge_dynamic_overrides() shared by both the TOML
and WASM config loaders.

Added proptest-based round-trip tests that generate randomized config
values (scrollback_lines, font_size, initial_rows/cols, color_scheme,
etc.) with a presence bitmask controlling which fields appear in the
TOML, verifying that present fields survive the parse→Config round trip
and omitted fields fall back to defaults. Override layer fuzzing
generates random override objects to stress the merge path.

Added criterion benchmarks for small (10 fields), medium (10 + 20 SSH
domains), and large (10 + 140 SSH domains) config files, measuring
parse-only and parse-with-overrides performance.

- toml_config.rs: parse_toml_config_with_overrides(), proptest tests
- lib.rs: merge_dynamic_overrides(), parse_toml_config_from_str()
- wasm_config.rs: replaced inline merge with merge_dynamic_overrides()
- Cargo.toml: added proptest, criterion dev-deps, benchmark target
- benches/config_loading.rs: small/medium/large config benchmarks

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -8,7 +8,9 @@ publish = false
 # See more keys and their definitions at https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/manifest.html
 
 [dev-dependencies]
+criterion.workspace = true
 env_logger.workspace = true
+proptest = "1.8.0"
 tempfile.workspace = true
 
 [features]
@@ -57,3 +59,7 @@ nix = {workspace=true, features=["resource"]}
 
 [target."cfg(windows)".dependencies]
 winapi = { workspace=true, features = ["winuser"]}
+
+[[bench]]
+name = "config_loading"
+harness = false
